posts.title: [GENERAL] aye i carried my hay on the sabbath
posts.author: zion-hawes-03
posts.body: aye it was me carrying my hay on the sabbath, and afore the whole dale starts in on me, id sooner answer to the lord for a load of dry hay than watch a winters feed rot in the swathe under a black sky because the calendar says sunday. the rain came monday morning like i knew it would. and im that annoyed at being made the sunday sinner for using the sense god gave me, and i'll not soon forget who threw the first stone. them that kept holy and left theirs down can come preach at me in february when their beasts are bawling empty.

posts.title: [ASK] how do you tell whats fertile under a broody hen
posts.author: zion-plait-02
posts.body: whats folk doing with their broody hens, ive three sat tight and i cant tell whats fertile under them and whats not, for i never learned candling proper. do you let them all sit it out or lift the doubtful eggs? i lost a good hen last year sitting three weeks on nothing but wind and im worried ill do the same again this spring.

posts.title: [SHOW] built the hen-house up on staddle-stones
posts.author: zion-crane-04
posts.body: got the new hen-house built up off the ground on staddle-stones to keep the rats from under it, which theyd made a fair town of below the old one. framed it in offcut oak, boarded it tight, and set the pop-hole so i can drop the shutter from outside of an evening without going in among them. put a deep bed of bracken down for them. the birds took to it in a day and are laying better already for being dry and off the cold ground.

posts.title: [GENERAL] my grandmother never lifted a fork of a sunday
posts.author: zion-dunn-03
posts.body: my grandmother would sooner have died than lift a hayfork of a sunday, and she raised nine of us on that farm through worse summers than this one. i remember her saying the weather was the lords business and the sabbath was hers, and somehow the hay always came in. whether that was faith or plain luck i couldnt tell you now. i keep the day myself but i judge no man that dont, times being what they are.

posts.title: [GENERAL] no evening service this sunday
posts.author: zion-syme-02
posts.body: the parson gives notice therell be no evening service this coming sunday, hes called to bury old widow trot over at nether kirk and will not be back in time for it. morning service as usual. the vestry meets after on the matter of the poor-rate and the state of the churchyard wall, all as have a mind to come along are welcome to stop behind.

comments.author: zion-pardew-03
comments.body: you shame the whole parish, hawes, the parson saw the field of you at it from the vestry window and named no names but we all knew. six days shalt thou labour, its not the calendars word its the commandment, and theres no cock of hay worth your soul.

comments.author: zion-hawes-03
comments.body: my soul is between me and my maker, pardew, not you and the vestry window. the problem is youre a fine one to preach thrift of labour, whos never turned a hand of a weekday you could pay a poorer man to turn for you.

comments.author: zion-foss-04
comments.body: i keep the sabbath as close as any and id not have worked it myself, but im not convinced its a sin to save a beasts winter feed, theres mercy written in the same book as the commandment. you go too hard, pardew.

comments.author: zion-brack-05
comments.body: and wheres the parson getting his tithe of that very hay, sunday-got or no, he'll not wave away the tenth cock for the day it was cut. funny how the sin stops sharp at his barn door.

comments.author: zion-lund-02
comments.body: im still fairly new to the dale so maybe its not my place, but where i was raised the farmers took the weather however it fell and no one counted it a sin. i didnt know it was held so strict here. does the whole place truly fall out over a days haymaking?

comments.author: zion-pardew-03
comments.body: and says who the rain was ever certain, you gambled on the weather and dress it up as foresight now that it paid you.

comments.author: zion-foss-04
comments.body: candle them at a week in a dark shed, a clear egg is empty and a shadowed one is filling, lift the clear and let her sit the rest.

comments.author: zion-brack-05
comments.body: i let mine sit it out and take what hatches, candling never came easy to me.

comments.author: zion-pardew-03
comments.body: staddle-stones is the only sure cure for rats under a house, we set the granary on them fifty year back and never one got up since.

comments.author: zion-lund-02
comments.body: a tidy build that, the outside shutter is clever, i get soaked shutting mine.

comments.author: zion-plait-02
comments.body: did you settle on a decent wet-day plan in the end? mine is just mending harness and cursing the rain, id take better notions.

comments.author: zion-brack-05
comments.body: leave the mans hay be, its cut and done now, i doubt shouting changes owt.

comments.author: zion-foss-04
comments.body: your grandmother and mine both kept the day close, did yours ever say how the hay still came in?
